nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: Working with AI feels like that - like we are a team working together to make something special

nodes.label: AI predictability and consistency
nodes.label_negative: AI output with artifacts (e.g., six fingers)
nodes.grounding_span: anything AI-generated has to look real - characters have to be consistent, people can't have 6 fingers

nodes.label: AI integration invisibility
nodes.grounding_span: stitch together some stills we shot with footage we took by using AI, without it looking like I'm using AI

nodes.label: AI as learning tool despite heavy lifting
nodes.grounding_span: I spend a lot of time learning - or figuring out - how to use different tools to accomplish what I want

nodes.label: AI as practical labor-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as job creator or neutral
nodes.grounding_span: as companies figure out how to use these tools, they will need less people to do the creative work

nodes.label: Creative satisfaction and enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: when I get a good result, I am very happy

nodes.label: Seamless visual storytelling
nodes.grounding_span: trying to get a smooth transition has been very interesting

nodes.label: Job security and skill relevance
nodes.grounding_span: they will need less people to do the creative work

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: when I get a good result, I am very happy

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's agreeableness
nodes.grounding_span: trying to make that one fix will set off a whole slew of other problems

nodes.label: Concern about job displacement
nodes.grounding_span: they will need less people to do the creative work

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: The video side is coming along faster than that

nodes.label: Iterative experimentation and trial-and-error
nodes.grounding_span: I'm going to have to just run a hundred variations of the same thing before I get one perfect generation

nodes.label: Historical pattern recognition across career stages
nodes.grounding_span: It's not really any different than the past couple decades have been though
